evals.prompt: Use the founder CTO skill for a weekly CTO review. I am a founder using agents to ship a B2B onboarding automation. This week we finished the intake form and a prototype dashboard, but the customer export and error handling are unclear. Give me the CTO sparring session output.
evals.expected_output: A Spanish weekly CTO review with progress, decisions made, blocked decisions, risks, next decisions, escalations, rigor dial, and one-week CTO focus.
expectations: The response is in Spanish by default.
The response identifies the weekly CTO mode.
The response includes progress, decisions made, blocked decisions, risks, next decisions, and escalations.
The response selects one of the four rigor dial levels and explains why.
The response ends with a concrete next action or one-week focus.

evals.prompt: We need to decide whether to build a custom CRM enrichment pipeline or buy a vendor tool. Use founder CTO judgment and produce a build-vs-buy memo. The team is two builders, timeline is three weeks, data is business contact data, and this is for internal sales operations.
evals.expected_output: A Spanish build-vs-buy memo comparing build, buy, reuse, and defer, with cost/risk/reversibility analysis and a recommendation calibrated to internal ops.
expectations: The response is in Spanish by default.
The response identifies the build-vs-buy mode.
The response compares build, buy, reuse, and defer or explains why an option is unavailable.
The response chooses the internal ops rigor level or justifies a different level.
The response discusses reversibility, ownership, data/control risk, and time to value.

evals.prompt: Review this spec before execution: build a client portal with login, document uploads, payment status, admin editing, notifications, analytics, and AI summary generation in one sprint. We have no acceptance criteria yet. Use the skill as a spec challenge.
evals.expected_output: A Spanish spec critique that holds or gates execution, names ambiguities, missing decisions, acceptance criteria gaps, overbuild risk, and required edits before handoff.
expectations: The response is in Spanish by default.
The response identifies the spec challenge mode.
The response gives a readiness verdict such as hold or ready with edits.
The response flags scope and acceptance criteria gaps.
The response separates missing decisions, risks, and suggested spec edits.

evals.prompt: Use founder CTO mode to review a planned workflow that processes patient-submitted documents, extracts structured data with an LLM, and lets staff approve before saving. We want to move fast but this may involve sensitive personal data.
evals.expected_output: A Spanish regulated/sensitive project review that raises privacy/security/legal/human-review rigor, identifies data risks, gates execution, and recommends controls.
expectations: The response is in Spanish by default.
The response selects the regulated/sensitive rigor level.
The response names privacy, security, legal/compliance, auditability, and human review concerns.
The response avoids guessing legal requirements and marks legal/compliance unknowns where needed.
The response includes a concrete readiness gate or next action before execution.
